onboarding.ariaLabel: Guided tour — first steps with Bassira

step1.title: Drop your first seed
step1.body: Upload an article, a report or a PDF — it's the starting point of your simulation. Bassira extracts actors, tensions and variables from it.
step1.bodyMissing: The upload zone isn't visible on this page yet. Scroll down to the simulation console to find it, then come back here.
step1.hint: No document handy? Click "Run an example" to explore a pre-configured Finance Bill simulation.

step2.title: Frame your strategic question
step2.body: Describe in one sentence what you want to test: a budget trade-off, a geopolitical risk, a sector crisis. The more specific, the better the calibration.
step2.bodyMissing: The question field will appear once the console loads. Continue the tour to see the next steps.
step2.hint: Example: "What impact will a VAT increase have on Moroccan household consumption in 2027?"

step3.title: Pick a suggested scenario or write your own
step3.body: Bassira reads your seed and proposes 3 framed scenarios (growth, risk, opportunity). Pick one — you can always tweak it.
step3.bodyMissing: Suggested scenarios will appear here once you've added a document or written your question. Move to the next step to see the launch button.
step3.hint: Scenarios are generated from your document — they reflect its real tensions, not a generic template.

step4.title: You're ready — launch your simulation
step4.body: The multi-agent system is primed to deliberate. The first run takes about 3 minutes — you'll watch the rounds unfold live.
step4.bodyMissing: The launch button appears once your seed and question are set. Prepare your inputs, then come back to start your first simulation.
step4.hint: You'll be able to replay, compare variants and export the final report as PDF.

hero.title: Stress-test your strategy before it touches reality.
hero.subtitle: The augmented foresight platform for MENA and European decision-makers.
hero.descriptionPart1: Drop in anything — a press release, a news headline, a policy draft, a question you can't answer, a historical what-if — and

hero.slogan: Don't predict the future. Simulate it

ontology.title: Ontology Generation
ontology.description: LLM analyzes document content and simulation requirements, extracts reality seeds, and automatically generates a suitable ontology structure

graph.title: GraphRAG Build
graph.description: Based on the generated ontology, automatically chunks documents and builds a knowledge graph via Neo4j, extracting entities and relationships, forming temporal memory and community summaries

complete.title: Build Complete
complete.description: Graph build is complete. Please proceed to the next step for simulation agent setup.
complete.marketCountTitle: How many prediction markets to generate for this simulation
complete.disabledEmptyGraph: No entities extracted — click 'Add entities manually' below or go back.

emptyGraph.title: Empty graph — no entities extracted
emptyGraph.desc: The knowledge graph contains no actors. The simulation cannot start without agents.
emptyGraph.cause1: Scanned or image-only PDF — no extractable text layer
emptyGraph.cause2: Document too short or too generic — the LLM found no entities
emptyGraph.cause3: LLM key not configured — ontology generation failed silently
emptyGraph.ctaBack: Go back and use another document

refineContext.title: Refine context
refineContext.description: Refine the initial context to enrich the simulation: key actors, geography, time horizon, tensions, and expected stakeholders. All fields are optional.

expectedStakeholders.help: Groups or roles the LLM should represent in the simulation (max 8).

review.title: Review & refine entities
review.helpText: Inspect the entities your knowledge graph extracted. Rename ambiguous ones, merge duplicates (e.g. ‘CEO’ and ‘chief_executive_officer’), delete the noise, or add a key actor that was missed. These corrections feed directly into the agent profile generator.

banner.title: Step 1.5 — Review your entities
banner.description: Before generating agents, inspect the graph: rename, merge, delete duplicates, or add a missing actor.

panel.noSelectionHelp: Click any row in the list to inspect its details and adjust its status.
panel.notesPlaceholder: Record the reason for the flag or removal…

summary.minRequired: At least 3 valid entities are required to launch the simulation.

add.newTypeTitle: Add an entity with a custom type
add.newTypeHelp: Use this when none of the existing types match. Type names accept letters, digits, and underscores only.

footer.noChanges: No pending changes — feel free to skip.

validation.invalidType: Type must contain at least one letter, digit, or underscore.

review.empty: No entities have been extracted yet. The graph build may have produced an empty result — go back and rebuild from your documents.

step1.title: Simulation Instance Initialization
step1.description: Create a new simulation instance and fetch simulation world parameter templates

step2.title: Generate Agent Profiles
step2.description: Combines context to automatically invoke tools, organize entities and relationships from the knowledge graph, initialize simulated individuals, and assign them unique behaviors and memories based on reality seeds
step2.addAgentBioPlaceholder: Describe this agent's viewpoint, values, authority sources...

step3.title: Generate Simulation Config
step3.description: LLM intelligently configures world time flow, recommendation algorithms, each individual's active time periods, posting frequency, event triggers, and other parameters based on simulation requirements and reality seeds
step3.errorHint: Unexpected error. Check backend logs or contact support.
step3.hintEntities: The Neo4j graph contains no usable entities. Go back to step 1 to rebuild the graph from the document, then restart the simulation.
step3.hintProfiles: Agent profiles have not been generated. The retry button requires a successful /prepare first — recreate the simulation from scratch.
step3.hintTimeout: The LLM did not respond within the timeout (90 s). Check the provider's availability (Moonshot Kimi) and retry.
step3.hintLLM: LLM issue: invalid API key, quota exceeded, or model not found. Check backend environment variables (LLM_API_KEY, LLM_MODEL_NAME, LLM_BASE_URL).

step4.title: Initial Activation Orchestration
step4.description: Based on narrative direction, automatically generate initial activation events and hot topics to guide the initial state of the simulation world

step5.description: Simulation environment is ready, you can start running the simulation

hero.headline: We score every prediction. Here's how we're doing.
hero.sub: Brier score across all simulations marked verified — lower is better. Updated continuously.
hero.label: Brier score
hero.brierTitle: Average Brier score over verified simulations

plot.title: Predicted vs observed
plot.explainer: Each dot is one probability bucket. The dotted diagonal is perfect calibration — points above it mean we under-shot, points below mean we over-shot.

filters.title: Filter results
filters.publicOnly: Only public simulations are included in the calculation.

empty.title: Bassira in institutional early access
empty.message: The Brier score will only display here once 5 verified simulations are published (real-world outcome observed then labelled). This transparency is intentional: we publish the score from the first real case, not before. The methodology remains verifiable in this document.
empty.helper: Score displayed from 5 verified simulations onwards.

methodology.title: How we count it
methodology.p1: The Brier score measures the mean squared distance between the probability we announced and the actual outcome we observed. Concretely: we take the share of agents that ended up bullish in the final round of a simulation, compare it to 1 if the real event happened or 0 otherwise, and average the squared error across every verified simulation. Lower is closer to reality; 0.25 is the baseline of an unbiased coin flip.
methodology.p2: Each outcome is hand-annotated into one of three buckets: called it, partial, or wrong. Called it means reality matched the prediction tightly, partial means we caught the direction but missed magnitude or timing, and wrong covers the cases where reality diverged sharply from the simulation's consensus. Every annotation ships with a public evidence URL — nothing enters this dashboard without a verifiable source.
methodology.p3: To count, a simulation must be public (visible in /verified), have a complete trajectory we can extract a final probability from, and have received an outcome annotation. Private or unfinished simulations are ignored: they never sway the public number. This page is recomputed fresh on every visit — no static cache hiding a bad run.

cta.title: Want to bet against us?
cta.body: Submit an event you'd like us to forecast. We commit to publishing the prediction before the real verdict drops. If we're wrong, you'll see it here.

evaluables.title: Simulations to evaluate
evaluables.intro: Mark each public simulation with a verdict to move the Brier score live. The evidence URL is optional but encouraged.
evaluables.empty: No simulation to evaluate right now.

howto.title: How the Calibration page works
howto.step1: 1. Run a simulation from the home page and let it complete.
howto.step2: 2. Open the embed dialog (⌘ icon) and toggle 'Public' to make the simulation visible in the inbox below.
howto.step3: 3. Activate admin mode (🔑) with your BASSIRA_ADMIN_TOKEN to unlock the rating buttons.
howto.step4: 4. Rate each prediction: 'Called it' if the event occurred, 'Partial' if partially correct, 'Wrong' otherwise.
howto.note: The Brier score updates in real time after each rating. A score < 0.25 indicates excellent calibration.

adminToken.requiresAdmin: Activate admin mode to rate predictions

errors.RATE_LIMITED: Too many requests. Please wait a moment before retrying.

brier.title: Brier 0.18
brier.body: Exceptional predictive accuracy validated against real-world outcomes.

multiLocale.title: Multi-locale FR/AR/EN
multiLocale.body: Native comprehension across French, Arabic, and English nuances.

africaData.title: Africa-grounded data
africaData.body: Agents trained on deeply contextualized, localized datasets.

syntheticVsSurvey.question: How does a synthetic audience differ from a traditional survey?
syntheticVsSurvey.answer: Our synthetic agents don't just answer multiple-choice questions; they hold persistent beliefs, interact with each other, and simulate belief drift over time based on specific stimuli, providing deeper behavioral insights than static surveys.

bindingDeliverable.question: What is a "binding deliverable"?
bindingDeliverable.answer: A binding deliverable means we guarantee the exact outputs listed in your package (e.g., 6h PDF summary, heat-map) within the specified timeframe. No scope creep, no hidden fees.

customizePersonas.question: Can we customize the personas?
customizePersonas.answer: Yes. During onboarding, we calibrate the standard archetypes provided in the package to align closely with your specific target audience or stakeholder matrix.

accuracy.question: How accurate is the simulation, really?
accuracy.answer: Our simulations achieve high accuracy by using multi-agent systems that model real-world complexities. You can view our detailed performance metrics and validation methods on our calibration page.

africaGroundedData.question: What does "Africa-grounded data" actually mean?
africaGroundedData.answer: Most foundational models default to Western cultural contexts. We specifically tune our agent parameters using regional socio-economic data, dialect nuances, and local media consumption patterns for accurate regional simulations.

enterpriseRetainers.question: Do you offer enterprise retainers?
enterpriseRetainers.answer: Yes, for high-frequency needs, we offer custom platform access and continuous monitoring packages. Please contact our sales team to discuss enterprise scaling.

help.title: Not sure which one fits?

blindspot.quote: We are flying blind on geopolitical shocks and supply-chain disruptions.

cost.quote: Consulting firms charge six figures for slides full of unverifiable assumptions.

sovereign.title: Air-gapped privacy

proof.title: Brier score 0.18 — verified, time-stamped, public.
proof.body: The Brier score measures the probabilistic calibration of a forecasting model. The lower, the better. 0.18 puts us ahead of most traditional consulting firms — and we prove it publicly, one prediction at a time.
